# Solar Monitor — MVP Design

**Author:** Marius Bloemhof
**Date:** 2026-05-02
**Status:** Working design (post-brainstorm). Drives the implementation plan generated by `superpowers:writing-plans`.
**Linear project:** [Solar Monitor MVP](https://linear.app/solamon/project/solar-monitor-mvp-e5dcb9373fbc)

---

## 1. Executive summary

Deliver a working POC in **2-4 weeks** that lets Johan Wolmarans install an **AccuEnergy Acuvim L meter + Raspberry Pi 5 with LTE** at a prospective client site, capture the client's energy usage and load profile in the cloud, render it in a Next.js dashboard for sales engineering, and demonstrate end-to-end remote control by flipping the meter's demand integration period from a web button.

**Strangler-fig posture:**

- **Foundation-grade** (the eventual full system inherits these directly): data shape, ingestion contract, MQTT topic structure, FastAPI surface, Next.js + React + Tailwind UI codebase, Postgres + TimescaleDB schema, the device-library architecture (logical metrics + per-variant device profiles).
- **Pragmatic / replaceable** (knowingly throwaway, with planned migrations on the dashboard): single EC2 + Docker compose, Mosquitto MQTT broker, Python edge agent, Tailscale for Pi management, single shared admin user, single device per Pi.

**Three planned migrations on the dashboard** (tracked in [SOL-10](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-10)):

| Layer | MVP | Full system |
|-------|-----|-------------|
| Edge agent | Python (`pymodbus` + `paho-mqtt`) | Go |
| Cloud | Single EC2 + Docker compose, af-south-1 | AWS-native: IoT Core, Lambda, ECS Fargate |
| Pi management | Tailscale | AWS SSM |

---

## 2. System shape

```
                  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
                  │  Operator (Marius / Johan) laptop        │
                  │  - HTTPS to web UI + API                 │
                  │  - Tailscale SSH to Pi for ops           │
                  └───────────────┬──────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  │ HTTPS (Caddy → Next.js / FastAPI)
                                  ▼
              ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
              │  AWS af-south-1 · single EC2 (t4g.small, ARM64)   │
              │                                                    │
              │   Docker compose:                                  │
              │   ┌─────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐    │
              │   │  Caddy  │ │  Next.js UI  │ │  FastAPI    │    │
              │   │  (TLS)  │ │              │ │  + ingest   │    │
              │   └─────────┘ └──────────────┘ │  + control  │    │
              │                                 └──────┬──────┘    │
              │                                        │           │
              │   ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────▼──────┐   │
              │   │  Postgres +      │◄─│  Single Python proc │   │
              │   │  TimescaleDB     │  │  (FastAPI + workers)│   │
              │   └──────────────────┘  └──────────┬──────────┘   │
              │                                    │              │
              │                          ┌─────────▼──────────┐   │
              │                          │  Mosquitto (MQTT)  │   │
              │                          │  TLS on 8883       │   │
              │                          └─────────┬──────────┘   │
              └────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┘
                                                   │
                                  MQTT/TLS over LTE (8883)
                                                   │
              ┌────────────────────────────────────▼──────────────┐
              │  Raspberry Pi 5 (LTE)  ·  bench / client site     │
              │                                                    │
              │   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
              │   │  Edge agent (Python, Docker container)   │    │
              │   │  · Profile loader  · pymodbus poller     │    │
              │   │  · paho-mqtt publisher + subscriber      │    │
              │   │  · SQLite ring buffer (7-day)            │    │
              │   │  · Tailscale agent (sidecar)             │    │
              │   └──────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
              │                       │                            │
              │                       │ Modbus TCP :502 (LAN)      │
              │                       ▼                            │
              │   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
              │   │  AccuEnergy Acuvim L (CL/DL/EL/KL)       │    │
              │   └──────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
              └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 3. Locked decisions

| Layer | Choice | One-line rationale |
|-------|--------|---------------------|
| Cloud region | AWS `af-south-1` (Cape Town), greenfield account | POPIA-clean by default; mature services; SA developer ecosystem |
| Cloud compute | Single `t4g.small` EC2 + Docker compose | Fastest path; everything debuggable from one SSH session |
| Database | Postgres 16 + TimescaleDB extension, two schemas (`timeseries`, `app`) | Single DB for time-series + relational; SQL JOINs across telemetry and billing |
| MQTT broker | Mosquitto in Docker | Free, simple, hostname-replaceable when we move to IoT Core |
| API + ingestion + control relay | Single FastAPI process (Python) with background tasks | Three less containers to debug; foundation-grade Python lives in the full system |
| Web UI | Next.js 15 (App Router) + React + Tailwind + shadcn/ui + **Tremor** for charts | Foundation-grade — carries forward unchanged into full system |
| TLS / proxy | Caddy with Let's Encrypt | Auto-provisioning; zero TLS config |
| Edge agent language | **Python** (`pymodbus`, `paho-mqtt`); planned Go port (SOL-10) | Fastest learning curve given Modbus / energy domain unknowns |
| Edge agent supervision | Docker container, restart policy `always` | Deployed and updated like the cloud services |
| Local buffer | SQLite WAL, 7-day ring (~140 MB) | Survives reboot; replays on reconnect |
| Pi management | Tailscale; planned AWS SSM (SOL-10) | Five minutes per Pi, no inbound ports, MagicDNS, removable later |
| Pi → MQTT auth | Per-site username/password over TLS:8883 | mTLS deferred until IoT Core migration |
| Browser → cloud auth | Single shared admin user, bcrypt + JWT | Multi-user RBAC deferred (covered in SOL-8 sub-tickets) |
| Pi base image | Raspberry Pi OS Lite (64-bit) + custom `solamon-edge-install.sh` | Tight, Docker-only, no IOTstack bloat |
| Charts | Tremor (cards, metrics, line charts); bare Recharts where Tremor doesn't fit | Purpose-built for analytics dashboards; cohesive look |
| Forms | React Hook Form + Zod | Standard, type-safe, integrates with shadcn/ui |
| API client | OpenAPI codegen → typed TypeScript SDK | FastAPI auto-generates the spec; no hand-written endpoint typings |
| Live data | WebSocket from FastAPI (forwards from MQTT subscription) | No Redis in MVP; single-process subscribe + forward |
| Container registry | AWS ECR Private (af-south-1) | Co-located with the cloud account; free at our scale |
| Site count in MVP | 1 (bench, then prospect site) | Multi-site infra is in the data model; UI shows one site at a time |
| Devices per Pi in MVP | 1 (Acuvim L only) | Multi-device deferred (SOL-16) |

---

## 4. Edge agent

### 4.1 Process model

Single Python process running as a Docker container, supervised by Docker (`restart: always`). One `asyncio` event loop with three concurrent tasks:

- **Modbus poller** — reads each profile-defined block at its configured cadence; writes readings to local SQLite + queues for publish.
- **MQTT publisher** — publishes new readings to cloud broker; on broker ack, marks SQLite rows `published=true`. Replay sweeper drains unpublished rows oldest-first every 30 s.
- **Command subscriber** — subscribes to `solamon/{site}/commands/{device_id}`; on message, validates against profile, executes Modbus FC06 write, waits 500 ms, reads back, publishes ack to `commands/{device_id}/ack`.

Heartbeat task (60 s) publishes to `solamon/{site}/heartbeat` with `{ timestamp, edge_version, buffer_depth_seconds }`. LWT publishes `{ status: offline }` on unexpected disconnect.

### 4.2 Configuration

- Site config (`site_config.yaml`) cached in `/etc/solamon/`, fetched from cloud at startup via `GET /api/v1/edge/config/{site_slug}`. Falls back to local cache if cloud unreachable.
- Logical metric catalog and the device profile for the configured `device_model` are bundled into the same response.
- Config changes today require `systemctl restart solamon-edge` (or `docker compose restart edge-agent`). Hot reload deferred to [SOL-17](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-17).

### 4.3 Polling cadences (Acuvim L)

| Block | Address | Cadence | Notes |
|-------|---------|---------|-------|
| Real-time block | `0x0600`–`0x0649` (Float32 BE, 74 regs) | **10 s** | One batched FC03; covers V/I/P/Q/S/PF/F per phase + total |
| Demand sub-block | within `0x063E`–`0x0649` | stored at **30 s** (read at 10s, downsampled) | Active/reactive/apparent demand + per-phase current demand |
| Energy counters | `0x0156`–`0x015F` (Dword × 0.1) | **60 s** | Import/export/reactive/apparent energy |
| THD totals | `0x0400`–`0x0405` | **60 s** | THD V1/V2/V3, THD I1/I2/I3 |
| Peak demand stats | `0x1024`–`0x1027`, `0x1038`–`0x103B` | **300 s** | Max demand + timestamp |
| Clock | `0x0184`–`0x018A` | **3600 s** | Drift detection vs Pi NTP |
| Wiring config | `0x0103`–`0x010C` | startup + hourly | PT/CT ratios, wiring type, demand window |

Empirical confirmation of `0x0600` vs `0x4000` happens on Day 3 of bench rehearsal via `solamon-probe`; the cadence/scope above assumes the documented `0x0600` block is canonical.

### 4.4 Local buffer

SQLite WAL DB at `/var/lib/solamon/buffer.db`. One row per reading: `(time, device_id, logical_metric_key, value, raw_value, quality, published)`. Background sweeper deletes rows older than 7 days every 10 minutes. Volume estimate: ~1.7 M rows / week / device ≈ 140 MB.

### 4.5 Control execution

1. Receive command JSON on `solamon/{site}/commands/{device_id}` (QoS 1).
2. Validate command type + parameters against profile's `control:` block (e.g., `set_demand_window` allowed values `[1, 5, 10, 15, 30]`).
3. Modbus FC06 write to the configured control register.
4. Sleep 500 ms.
5. Modbus FC03 read of the same register.
6. Compare read-back to intended value.
7. Publish ack to `commands/{device_id}/ack` with `{ id, status, confirmed_value, acknowledged_at, error_message }` where status ∈ `{ acknowledged, confirmed, failed, expired }`.

---

## 5. Cloud stack

### 5.1 Docker compose layout (single EC2)

```yaml
services:
  caddy:                  # TLS terminator; reverse-proxies / + /api → next + fastapi
  postgres:               # postgres:16-alpine + TimescaleDB extension; pgdata volume
  mosquitto:              # eclipse-mosquitto:2; TLS:8883; password + ACL files
  app:                    # FastAPI + ingestion worker + control relay (single Python process)
  web:                    # Next.js standalone build
```

Daily `pg_dump` to S3 (af-south-1) via cron in the postgres container or a sidecar.

### 5.2 MQTT topic structure

```
solamon/{site}/telemetry/{device_id}              ← Pi → cloud (one msg per read block, QoS 1)
solamon/{site}/heartbeat                          ← Pi → cloud (60 s + LWT)
solamon/{site}/commands/{device_id}               ← cloud → Pi (control commands, QoS 1)
solamon/{site}/commands/{device_id}/ack           ← Pi → cloud (read-back ack, QoS 1)
solamon/{site}/config_changed                     ← cloud → Pi (post-MVP hot-reload signal — SOL-17)
```

**Telemetry payload:**
```json
{
  "site_slug": "johansworkbench",
  "device_id": "uuid",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-02T12:34:56.789Z",
  "block": "realtime",
  "source": "modbus_poll",
  "quality": "good",
  "readings": {
    "frequency_hz": 50.02,
    "voltage_l1_n": 231.4,
    "active_power_total": 12.350,
    "...": "..."
  }
}
```

**Command payload (cloud → Pi):**
```json
{
  "id": "cmd-uuid",
  "type": "set_demand_window",
  "parameters": { "window_minutes": 30 },
  "issued_at": "...",
  "issued_by": "user-uuid",
  "expires_at": "..."
}
```

**Ack payload (Pi → cloud):**
```json
{
  "id": "cmd-uuid",
  "status": "confirmed",
  "confirmed_value": { "window_minutes": 30 },
  "acknowledged_at": "...",
  "error_message": null
}
```

### 5.3 Mosquitto auth & ACL

Per-site username/password (auto-generated 32-byte secret). ACL file partitions topics by site:

```
user solamon-bench-01
  topic write  solamon/bench-01/telemetry/#
  topic write  solamon/bench-01/heartbeat
  topic write  solamon/bench-01/commands/+/ack
  topic read   solamon/bench-01/commands/#
  topic read   solamon/bench-01/config_changed

user solamon-cloud
  topic readwrite solamon/#       # FastAPI subscribes for ingestion + publishes commands
```

---

## 6. Data model

MVP subset of [`architecture/data-model/DATA_MODEL.md`](../../architecture/data-model/DATA_MODEL.md), with a small refactor to support the device-library pattern.

### 6.1 Refactor: `Metric` → `LogicalMetric` + `DeviceProfile`

| Before (DATA_MODEL.md v0.1) | After (this MVP) |
|------------------------------|--------------------|
| `Metric` (per device type, with `device_type_id` and `register_address`) | `LogicalMetric` (vendor-agnostic; no `device_type_id`, no `register_address`) |
| (implicit profile baked into `Metric` rows) | `DeviceProfile` (separate; YAML-defined, JSON column on `DeviceType`) |
| `Reading.metric_key` | `Reading.logical_metric_key` |

`DATA_MODEL.md` will be updated to v0.2 to reflect this.

### 6.2 Entities instantiated for MVP

| Schema | Entity | MVP rows | Notes |
|--------|--------|----------|-------|
| `app` | `Organisation` | 1 | Johan's company |
| `app` | `Site` | 1-2 | Bench, then prospect site; `slug` drives MQTT topic prefix |
| `app` | `DeviceType` | 1 | `acuvim_l` (with `acuvim_l.yaml` profile attached); `acuvim_ii` future |
| `app` | `Device` | 1 per site | host, port, unit_id, device_type_id |
| `app` | `LogicalMetric` | ~30 | Seeded from `architecture/logical_metrics.yaml` |
| `app` | `DeviceProfile` | 1 | Seeded from `architecture/profiles/acuvim_l.yaml`; stored as JSON column on `DeviceType` |
| `app` | `User` | 1 | Shared admin |
| `app` | `SiteAccess` | 1 | admin → bench |
| `app` | `ControlCommand` | grows | Lifecycle: `pending → sent → acknowledged → confirmed/failed/expired` |
| `app` | `AuditEntry` | grows | Append-only; one row per command issue + per status transition |
| `app` | `DeviceSnapshot` | 1 per device | `metrics jsonb`; latest values per logical metric |
| `timeseries` | `Reading` | TimescaleDB hypertable | `(time, device_id, logical_metric_key, value, raw_value, quality)`; partition key `time`; index `(device_id, logical_metric_key, time DESC)` |

### 6.3 Deferred entities

`BillingMeter`, `EnergyInterval`, `EnergyAdjustment`, `Contract`, `Invoice`, `InvoiceLine`, `AlertRule`, `Alert`, `ClientUsageSummary`, `ControlPolicy` — none in MVP. Tables not created.

---

## 7. Device library

### 7.1 Three layers

```
LOGICAL METRIC CATALOG (vendor-agnostic, repo-versioned)
   └── frequency_hz, voltage_l1_n, active_power_total, neutral_current,
       import_active_energy_kwh, demand_window_minutes, ...
        ▲
   resolved by
        │
DEVICE PROFILE (per device variant — one YAML each)
   └── acuvim_l.yaml: active_power_total → 0x061C, Float32 BE
   └── (future) acuvim_ii.yaml: active_power_total → 0x4022, Float32 BE
        ▲
   used by
        │
EDGE AGENT
   └── Loads profile, schedules batched reads, decodes per profile,
       emits readings keyed by LOGICAL metric name. No code knows registers.
```

### 7.2 Profile YAML schema (excerpt)

```yaml
device:
  manufacturer: AccuEnergy
  model: Acuvim L

connection:
  protocol: modbus_tcp
  default_port: 502
  default_unit_id: 1

fingerprint:
  reads:
    - address: 0xC350
      length: 2
      expect_exception: 0x02       # SunSpec block ABSENT on Acuvim L
    - address: 0x0130
      length: 1
      format: uint16
      expected_range: [4500, 6500] # Hz × 100
  identifiers: []                  # No identity registers on Acuvim L; capture from label at commissioning

read_blocks:
  - name: realtime
    fc: 3
    address: 0x0600
    length: 74
    cadence_s: 10
    metrics:
      - { logical: frequency_hz,         offset: 0,  format: float32_be }
      - { logical: voltage_l1_n,         offset: 4,  format: float32_be }
      - { logical: active_power_total,   offset: 56, format: float32_be }
      - { logical: neutral_current,      offset: 28, format: float32_be }
      # ...
  - name: energy
    fc: 3
    address: 0x0156
    length: 10
    cadence_s: 60
    metrics:
      - { logical: import_active_energy_kwh, offset: 0, format: dword_high_first, scale: 0.1 }
      - { logical: export_active_energy_kwh, offset: 4, format: dword_high_first, scale: 0.1 }
  # ...

control:
  demand_window_minutes:
    fc: 6
    address: 0x010C
    format: word
    allowed_values: [1, 5, 10, 15, 30]
```

### 7.3 Acuvim L fingerprint strategy

The Acuvim L manual V2.0.3 documents **no identity registers** (no manufacturer / model / firmware / serial on the wire). We use a **negative fingerprint** — read `0xC350` (the SunSpec common-model sentinel that exists on Acuvim II but not on L) and expect Modbus exception `0x02`, combined with a positive capability probe at `0x0130` (frequency, secondary) or `0x0600` (frequency, primary). Serial number, firmware version, and Acuvim L sub-variant (`CL`/`DL`/`EL`/`KL`) are captured at commissioning from the device label or AXM-WEB2 web UI and stored on the `Device` record.

### 7.4 Profile / catalog distribution

For MVP both files live in the repo under `architecture/`:

- `architecture/logical_metrics.yaml`
- `architecture/profiles/acuvim_l.yaml`

Loaded into Postgres (`device_profiles` table, JSON column) at cloud-deploy time via a `seed-config` script. FastAPI serves the consolidated config at `GET /api/v1/edge/config/{site_slug}`.

Editor UI, validation tools, versioning, hot reload, per-site overrides — all deferred. See [SOL-11](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-11), [SOL-12](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-12), [SOL-13](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-13), [SOL-14](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-14), [SOL-15](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-15), [SOL-17](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-17).

---

## 8. Probe CLI (`solamon-probe`)

Standalone Python CLI tool that connects to a Modbus TCP device, fingerprints it, probes its register blocks, and reports findings. Used at every site commissioning to confirm the device matches the configured profile **before** the edge daemon goes live. ([SOL-18](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-18).)

**Two layers:**

1. **Identity fingerprint** — runs each profile's `fingerprint:` block; reports which profile matched.
2. **Register-block probe** — for the matched profile, attempts each read block, decodes per metric, sanity-checks against the logical metric's `expected_range`. Cross-checks alternative profiles' blocks (e.g., when on an Acuvim L, also tries the Acuvim II `0x4000` block) — answers Johan's "the II map works on the L" question definitively.

**Output:** human-readable report + machine-readable JSON via `--json`. Exit 0 on full match; 1 on any mismatch.

LAN discovery (`solamon-discover`) is deferred to [SOL-19](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-19).

---

## 9. Web UI

### 9.1 Stack

| Concern | Choice |
|---------|--------|
| Framework | Next.js 15 (App Router, React Server Components) |
| Styling | Tailwind |
| Component primitives | shadcn/ui |
| Charts & cards | Tremor (primary); bare Recharts where Tremor doesn't fit |
| Data fetching | TanStack Query (client) + RSC (initial) |
| Live updates | WebSocket via TanStack Query subscriptions |
| Forms | React Hook Form + Zod |
| API client | Auto-generated TypeScript SDK from FastAPI's OpenAPI spec |
| Auth | NextAuth (credentials provider) → FastAPI `/auth/login` → JWT |

### 9.2 Page inventory

```
/login                                        public
/                                             auth required → redirect to /sites/{first-slug}
/sites/{slug}                                 LOAD ASSESSMENT DASHBOARD (primary view)
/sites/{slug}/control                         CONTROL PANEL (demand window flip + audit list)
/sites/{slug}/devices/{device_id}             Device detail (deeper view)
/admin                                        Admin landing
/admin/profiles                               Read-only profile browser (editor = SOL-12)
/admin/sites                                  Site list + setup info
/admin/users                                  User list (MVP: just admin)
```

### 9.3 Load Assessment Dashboard layout

Detailed card layout in [`requirements/acuvim_mvp_register_scope.md`](../../requirements/acuvim_mvp_register_scope.md) §J. Summary:

- **Hero row:** Active power tile / Energy today / Demand
- **Per-phase row:** Voltages / Currents / Per-phase active power / Power factor (4 sign-aware tiles — different sign on one phase = CT polarity flipped, visible at a glance)
- **Power quality row:** V-unbalance %, I-unbalance %, Frequency gauge, THD card, Edge health card (Modbus errors/min, last read, Pi heartbeat, buffer depth)
- **Main chart:** Psum over time with selectable window + overlay toggles
- **Sidebar:** Site setup (PT/CT ratios, wiring type, demand window, last config-read time)

### 9.4 Live data flow

```
Pi publishes telemetry → Mosquitto → FastAPI MQTT subscriber (in-process)
                                       ↓
                                FastAPI WebSocket endpoint
                                       ↓
                                Browser TanStack Query cache update
                                       ↓
                                Tremor / Recharts re-render
```

No Redis in MVP — single FastAPI process subscribes to MQTT and forwards to per-device WebSocket connections. Add Redis when we need to scale beyond one API container.

---

## 10. Control flow state machine

```
                  Operator clicks "Apply: 30 min"
                              │
                              ▼
        ┌──────────── pending ─────────────┐
        │ FastAPI: validate, persist,       │
        │ publish to MQTT, update sent_at   │
        └──────────────────┬────────────────┘
                           ▼
        ┌──────────── sent ────────────────┐
        │ Pi receives via paho-mqtt        │
        └──────────────────┬───────────────┘
                           ▼
        ┌──── acknowledged ─────────────┐    ┌──── failed ───┐
        │ Pi: validate against profile, │    │ Modbus write  │
        │ Modbus FC06 write             │    │ failed; ack   │
        └──────────────────┬────────────┘    │ with reason   │
                           ▼                  └───────────────┘
        ┌──── confirmed ────────────────┐    ┌── expired ────┐
        │ Pi: read back, value matches, │    │ TTL exceeded  │
        │ ack with confirmed_value      │    │ (5 min);      │
        └───────────────────────────────┘    │ cloud reaps   │
                                              └───────────────┘
```

Every transition writes an `AuditEntry` row. Browser subscribes to `/commands/{id}/live` and re-renders on each transition.

---

## 11. Auth model

| Direction | Mechanism | MVP credentials | Future |
|-----------|-----------|-----------------|--------|
| Pi → MQTT broker | Username/password over TLS:8883 | One per site (e.g. `solamon-bench-01` / random 32-byte secret) | mTLS via AWS IoT Core (SOL-10) |
| Pi → REST (config fetch) | Bearer token (same secret) | Same per-site secret | Same migration |
| Browser → cloud | bcrypt-hashed password → JWT (24 h) | Single shared `admin@solamon` | Multi-user RBAC (deferred — SOL-8 sub-tickets) |
| Cloud-internal (Postgres etc.) | Postgres role + password in env vars | Per-service Postgres role | Same |

---

## 12. Bench rehearsal sequence (10 working days)

| Day | Focus | Key outputs |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| 1 | Cloud bring-up | AWS account, IAM/MFA, EC2 + Tailscale, Caddy + TLS, full docker-compose up, `/health` green |
| 2 | Pi bring-up | Pi OS Lite + Tailscale + `solamon-edge-install.sh`, smoke-test publish to cloud Mosquitto |
| 3 | **Empirical Acuvim test** via `solamon-probe` — read both `0x0600` and `0x4000` from the bench Acuvim L, sanity-check against multimeter | `acuvim_l.yaml` finalised, committed |
| 4-5 | Edge agent build | Profile loader + poller + buffer + publisher + control subscriber. Telemetry visible in raw Postgres. |
| 6 | Next.js scaffold | `/login` works; `/sites/{slug}` renders empty layout |
| 7-8 | Dashboard build | Tremor cards + main chart + edge-health card; live WebSocket updates |
| 9-10 | Control panel + harden | `/sites/{slug}/control` route, command issue + live status, audit table; failure-mode tests (kill LTE, kill containers, restart) |
| 11-12 | Buffer | Polish, observation, doc rehearsal findings |
| 13 | Field deploy | At prospect site; live telemetry from real C&I site |

**Procurement before Day 1:** Pi 5 (8 GB), 32+ GB SD, USB-C PSU, Ethernet cable, small unmanaged switch, LTE modem (Quectel EC25-A or similar with SA bands), MTN/Vodacom IoT SIM, Acuvim L from Johan.

---

## 13. Planned migrations

Tracked in [SOL-10](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-10).

| # | What | When triggered | Effort |
|---|------|---------------|--------|
| 1 | Edge agent: Python → Go | MVP stable on at least one site + scoping the second device class (SmartLogger, Sinexcel) | ~1 week port + 1 week harden |
| 2 | Cloud: single EC2 + Docker compose → AWS-native (IoT Core / ECS / Lambda) | Onboarding the second site, OR reliability requirements get formal | ~3-4 weeks; piecemeal possible |
| 3 | Pi management: Tailscale → AWS SSM | Audit/compliance becomes a real requirement, OR fleet exceeds ~10 Pis | ~½ day per Pi to register; one afternoon for IAM activation flow |

---

## 14. Out of scope (explicit MVP exclusions)

**Architectural** (deferred):

- Multi-site rendering (one site at a time in MVP; data model is multi-site)
- Multi-device-per-Pi → [SOL-16](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-16)
- AWS-native cloud → [SOL-10](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-10)
- Edge agent in Go → [SOL-10](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-10)
- Pi management via SSM → [SOL-10](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-10)
- HA / multi-region (snapshot-restorable DB only)
- Hot config reload → [SOL-17](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-17)
- Profile auto-detection at agent startup → [SOL-11](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-11)

**Features** (deferred):

- Billing engine, invoices, contracts, `EnergyInterval` ingestion
- Client portal (Tier 2 user model — operator-only in MVP)
- Alert engine + notifications
- Sub-metering, disconnect-on-non-payment, multi-currency, VAT line-items, demand-charge calculation
- Profile editor UI → [SOL-12](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-12)
- Per-device register override → [SOL-13](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-13)
- Profile validation/linting → [SOL-14](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-14)
- Profile versioning → [SOL-15](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-15)
- LAN discovery CLI → [SOL-19](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-19)
- White-labelling, mobile app, dark mode, theming

**Device coverage** (deferred):

- Acuvim II profile (until we encounter the hardware; Johan's two units are both Acuvim L)
- All non-Acuvim devices (Huawei SmartLogger, Sinexcel PWS1-500K, EPC CAB1000, Schneider PM5110, DSE8610, PPC, Sungrow MBB)
- Acuvim L `AL`/`BL` sub-variants (no comms port; not relevant)
- Per-phase energy registers, TOU energy + configuration, full Min/Max V&I statistics, individual harmonic orders, Digital I/O module registers

**Auth & compliance** (deferred):

- Multi-user accounts + RBAC
- mTLS for Pi → cloud → [SOL-10](https://linear.app/solamon/issue/SOL-10) migration to IoT Core
- POPIA-compliant DPA, SSO integration

**Operational** (deferred):

- Audit log browser UI
- Encrypted local SQLite buffer (LAN-isolated; not a real risk for MVP)
- Automated OTA firmware updates (`ssh pi && docker compose pull && up -d` is fine for one site)
- DB backup automation beyond a single daily cron snapshot to S3

---

## 15. Open questions

Awaiting Johan ([requirements/acuvim_mvp_register_scope.md §K](../../requirements/acuvim_mvp_register_scope.md)):

1. **Acuvim L sub-variant on the bench** — `CL` / `DL` / `EL` / `KL`? Captured at commissioning from device label (no register tells us). Mainly affects whether TOU registers will be populated.
2. **AXM-WEB2 module fitted?** Confirms native Ethernet path. Without it: MOXA NPort + RS485.
3. **Acuvim Modbus IP / port / unit ID** on the LAN. Defaults `192.168.1.254:502/unit 1`.
4. **Demand window currently configured** (1/5/10/15/30 min) — "before" reference for the control demo.
5. **PT/CT ratios** Johan has configured — we'll log on startup.
6. **DO/DI module fitted?** (AXM-IO11/IO12) — opens up a relay-click control demo if present, but our current demo target is `0x010C` (demand window), so this is informational.

Empirical (resolved on bench Day 3 via `solamon-probe`):

7. **Does `0x4000` block work on the Acuvim L?** Johan claims yes; AccuEnergy docs imply no. The probe reads both `0x0600` and `0x4000` and reports which (if not both) returns sensible values. Outcome dictates whether we lock the profile to `0x0600` (per spec) or note a firmware aliasing variant.

---
